edges.evidence: This 2020 study examines online trust-building mechanisms in peer-to-peer accommodation providers, directly building on earlier Airbnb work that analyzed how personal photos shape trust and reputation.
By modeling factors influencing trust and intention to use Airbnb across ASEAN countries, this paper builds on prior Airbnb trust research centered on reputation cues such as personal photos.
This paper extends earlier Airbnb trust research into the under-studied context of Indian travelers and adoption in a developing-country setting.
The study extends earlier work on profile-based trust cues in Airbnb by testing how endorsement design can mitigate racial discrimination in platform interactions.
By comparing trust dynamics for Airbnb and Uber during COVID-19, this paper extends foundational Airbnb trust research into crisis conditions and cross-platform comparison.
Its focus on how trust evolves in sharing-economy services during a crisis builds on prior work identifying specific online mechanisms that generate trust in peer-to-peer accommodation.
This review extends broader gamification research into the specific domain of climate-change engagement and behavior change.
The Ant Forest study empirically examines gamification for environmentally responsible behavior, directly engaging the agenda identified in the earlier climate-change gamification review.
By applying gamification to urban planning and future-city visualization, this paper extends climate- and sustainability-oriented gamification scholarship to a planning context.
This study extends gamification-for-sustainability research to primary-school sustainable mobility learning using a technology acceptance framework.
The paper extends smart-campus research by focusing specifically on satisfaction with campus public transit services within a university community.
As a 2025 review of algorithmic management in organizations, it builds on earlier empirical evidence about how algorithmic management practices affect workplace well-being in European organizations.

notes: Most additional links are conservative thematic continuities: a strong Airbnb trust/reputation cluster, a gamification-for-sustainability cluster, one smart-campus-to-campus-mobility extension, and one algorithmic-management review-to-empirical link; no clear contradictions were identifiable from the provided metadata alone.
